disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Previously independent older adult who has become bedbound and unable to manage basic self-care over roughly the last 48 hours, on a background of about two months of withdrawal.
trigger_question_examples: How have things changed for you recently?
What has the last couple of days been like?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I was getting by on my own before, but the last two days I've barely been out of bed. I can't really look after myself right now.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Has eaten and drunk very little for approximately two days.
trigger_question_examples: Have you been able to eat and drink?
When did you last have a proper drink of water?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Not really. I haven't felt like eating or drinking for a couple of days. Maybe a few sips here and there.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Unsure of the day and month; can say the year. Family/triage note this muddling is new over recent days.
trigger_question_examples: Do you know what day or month it is today?
Have you felt more muddled or forgetful than usual the last few days?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm not sure what day it is, or the month really. I know it's this year. It's all a bit of a blur lately.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Has not taken regular blood-pressure medication for at least three days.
trigger_question_examples: Have you been able to take your usual tablets?
When did you last take your blood pressure medication?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I haven't taken my blood pressure tablets for a few days. I just haven't been keeping up with them.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Reports feeling cold and has no working thermometer to check a temperature.
trigger_question_examples: Have you felt hot, cold, or feverish?
How does your body temperature feel to you?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I feel cold, actually. I can't remember the last time I felt properly warm. I don't have a thermometer.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Feels profoundly unseen and lonely in the community, has withdrawn from contact, and is reluctant to seek care for fear of being dismissed.
trigger_question_examples: What's been going on for you lately?
Who do you have around you day to day?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No one really sees me anymore. I stopped going out. I didn't want to bother anyone or be told I'm just old and in the way.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Marked weakness — cannot rise from a chair without pushing up with both arms.
trigger_question_examples: Are you able to stand up and walk on your own?
Can you get out of a chair without help?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can barely get up. I have to push off the arms of the chair with both hands, and even then I'm shaky.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Home environment shows accumulating mail, expired food in the fridge, and trip hazards in the hallway.
trigger_question_examples: How are you managing the house and meals?
Is there anything at home making it harder to get around safely?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The mail's piled up, there's food gone off in the fridge, and there's clutter in the hall I keep nearly tripping on. I've let it all go.
